column1: 1,Barrel Exports,Module Organization,"barrel, index, re-export, module","Use index.ts files to re-export public APIs from a directory, providing a clean import surface and hiding internal module structure.",Create an index.ts in each feature directory that explicitly re-exports only the public API.,Do not re-export everything with wildcard exports or expose internal implementation details through barrel files.,"// src/users/index.ts

2,Nullish Coalescing (??),Operators,"nullish, coalescing, null, undefined, default","Use ?? to provide default values only when the left operand is null or undefined, unlike || which also triggers on falsy values like 0 or empty string.",Use ?? when 0 or empty string are valid values that should not be replaced by a default.,Do not use || for default values when falsy values like 0 or empty string are meaningful.,"const port = process.env.PORT ?? 3000
// count is 10 only if userInput is null/undefined
// Bug: if PORT is '0', it becomes 3000
// Bug: if userInput is 0, it becomes 10
// Bug: if name is '', it becomes 'default'",https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Operators/Nullish_coalescing

3,Optional Chaining (?.),Operators,"optional, chaining, null, safe, access","Use ?. to safely access deeply nested properties without explicit null checks at each level. Returns undefined if any part of the chain is null or undefined.",Use optional chaining for accessing properties on objects that may be null or undefined.,Do not overuse optional chaining on properties that should always exist as it can mask bugs.,"interface User {
// Dangerous: no null check at all

4,as const Assertions,Type Safety,"const, assertion, literal, readonly, immutable","Use as const to narrow literal types and make objects/arrays deeply readonly. This enables TypeScript to infer the most specific types possible.",Use as const for configuration objects and string literal unions to get precise type inference.,Do not use as const on mutable data structures that need to be modified after creation.,"const ROLES = ['admin', 'editor', 'viewer'] as const
// CONFIG.api is type 'https://api.example.com', not string","const ROLES = ['admin', 'editor', 'viewer']
// CONFIG.api is type string -- loses specificity

5,satisfies Operator,Type Safety,"satisfies, type checking, inference, validation","Use the satisfies operator to validate that an expression matches a type while preserving the most specific inferred type, combining type safety with precise inference.",Use satisfies to validate object shapes while keeping narrow literal types for autocomplete and type checking.,Do not use type annotations (: Type) when you want to preserve literal type inference

8,Strict TypeScript Config,Configuration,tsconfig, strict, typescript, compiler, options","Enable strict mode and other safety-focused compiler options in tsconfig.json to catch more errors at compile time.",Enable strict mode and add additional safety checks like noUncheckedIndexedAccess and exactOptionalPropertyTypes.,Do not leave strict mode off or disable individual strict checks without a documented reason.,"// tsconfig.json

9,Environment Variable Validation (Zod),Configuration,"env, zod, validation, environment, config","Use Zod to validate and parse environment variables at startup so that missing or malformed values cause an immediate and clear failure.",Define an env schema with Zod and parse process.env at application startup. Export the validated config object.,Do not access process.env directly throughout the codebase without validation.,"import { z } from 'zod'
// env.PORT is number, env.DATABASE_URL is string -- fully typed","// Scattered across codebase with no validation
// Runtime crash later when dbUrl is undefined

10,Graceful Shutdown,Process Management,"shutdown, graceful, signal, cleanup, process","Handle SIGTERM and SIGINT signals to gracefully close connections and finish in-flight requests before the process exits.",Register shutdown handlers that close database connections and HTTP servers and set a timeout for forced exit.,Do not let the process crash without cleaning up resources or ignore shutdown signals.,"import { createServer } from 'node:http'
// process just dies on SIGTERM, dropping connections

11,Structured Logging (pino),Observability,"logging, pino, structured, json, observability","Use a structured logger like pino that outputs JSON logs, making them parseable by log aggregation tools.",Use pino with context-specific child loggers that include relevant metadata like request ID and user ID.,Do not use console.log in production or log unstructured string messages that are hard to parse.,"import pino from 'pino'
const reqLogger = logger.child({ requestId: req.id, userId: user.id })
reqLogger.info({ path: req.url, method: req.method }, 'incoming request')
// Not parseable, no context, no levels, no correlation",https://getpino.io/

12,Early Return Pattern,Control Flow,"early return, guard clause, nesting, readability","Use early returns (guard clauses) to handle edge cases and error conditions first, reducing nesting and improving readability.",Check for invalid or edge-case conditions at the top of a function and return early.,Do not nest the main logic inside multiple if/else blocks.,"function processOrder(order: Order | null): Result {

13,Destructuring Best Practices,Syntax,"destructuring, object, array, parameters, clean","Use destructuring to extract values from objects and arrays concisely. Apply it to function parameters for clearer APIs.",Destructure function parameters and use meaningful variable names. Provide defaults in destructuring patterns.,Do not over-destructure deeply nested objects in a single statement or destructure more than needed.,"// Function parameter destructuring with defaults
const { name: userName, age: userAge } = user
const { data: { users: { edges: [{ node: { profile: { name } } }] } } } = response

14,Readonly and Immutability,Type Safety,readonly, immutable, freeze, const, mutation","Mark data that should not be mutated as Readonly<T> or use readonly properties. This documents intent and lets TypeScript enforce immutability.",Use Readonly<T> for function parameters that should not be mutated and readonly on class properties.,Do not mutate objects that are passed to your function unless the function's purpose is mutation.,"function processItems(items: readonly string[]): number {

15,Enum Alternatives (as-const objects),Type Safety,"enum, const, object, union, alternative","Prefer as-const objects or string literal unions over TypeScript enums. They are more predictable and produce less runtime code.",Use as const objects for groups of related constants and derive union types from them.,Do not use TypeScript enums unless you specifically need reverse mapping or are in an established enum-heavy codebase.,"const Status = {

16,Dependency Injection without Frameworks,Architecture,"dependency injection, DI, testing, decoupling, composition","Use constructor injection or factory functions to inject dependencies, making code testable and decoupled without needing a DI framework.",Pass dependencies as constructor or function parameters. Create factory functions that wire everything together.,Do not import and use dependencies directly in modules if it makes them hard to test or swap.,"// user.service.ts
if (!user) throw new Error('User not found')
const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { id } })
if (!user) throw new Error('User not found')

17,Configuration via Environment,Configuration,"config, environment, twelve-factor, env, settings","Centralize configuration by reading from environment variables and exporting a single typed config object. Follow the twelve-factor app methodology.",Create a single config module that reads and validates all environment variables and exports a typed object.,Do not scatter process.env access throughout business logic or hardcode configuration values.,"// config.ts

18,Path Aliases (tsconfig paths),Configuration,"paths, alias, tsconfig, import, module","Configure path aliases in tsconfig.json to avoid long relative import paths and make project structure changes easier.",Set up path aliases for major directories like src and use them consistently across the project.,Do not create too many aliases or use them inconsistently alongside relative imports.,"// tsconfig.json

20,Type-Only Imports (import type),Modules,"import type, type-only, erasure, bundle, optimization","Use import type for imports that are only used in type positions. This makes intent clear and can improve bundle size since type imports are fully erased.",Use import type when importing interfaces or types that are not used at runtime.,Do not use regular import for types that are only needed at compile time.,"import type { User, CreateUserDto } from './user.types'

21,Using unknown vs any,Type Safety,"unknown, any, type safety, narrowing, catch","Use unknown instead of any when the type is truly not known. unknown forces you to narrow the type before using it, maintaining type safety.",Use unknown for values of uncertain type and narrow with typeof or instanceof before use.,Do not use any as a shortcut to bypass type checking. Reserve any for legacy interop boundaries only.,"function parseInput(input: unknown): string {
throw new Error(`Unexpected input type: ${typeof input}`)

column1: 22,Narrowing with typeof/instanceof,Type Safety,"narrowing, typeof, instanceof, type guard, discriminated","Use typeof, instanceof, and discriminated unions to narrow types in conditional branches. TypeScript narrows the type automatically in each branch.",Use typeof for primitives and instanceof for class instances. Use discriminated unions for complex type narrowing.,Do not use type assertions (as Type) to bypass narrowing. Write proper type guards instead.,"// typeof narrowing
// Using type assertion instead of narrowing

23,Symbol for Unique Keys,Advanced,"symbol, unique, key, property, metadata","Use Symbol to create unique property keys that cannot clash with other properties. Useful for metadata, internal state, or framework-level properties.",Use Symbol for framework-internal keys or metadata that must not collide with user-defined properties.,Do not use string keys for internal metadata that could conflict with user data.,"const VALIDATION_KEY = Symbol('validation')
function markValidated<T extends object>(obj: T): T & Validatable {
// VALIDATION_KEY never collides with any string property","const VALIDATION_KEY = '__validation__'

25,Async IIFE for Scripts,Patterns,"async, iife, top-level, script, entrypoint","Use an async Immediately Invoked Function Expression (IIFE) to use await at the top level in scripts where top-level await is not available.",Wrap script logic in an async IIFE with proper error handling at the boundary.,Do not use .then().catch() chains at the top level or ignore errors from async entry points.,"// seed.ts
